methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $2.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Aalyria, a Series B space & aerospace company founded by Chris Taylor, closed its last round at a $1.3B post-money valuation after raising $150M+. The valuation analysis triangulates against 8 private peers in the same sector at comparable or adjacent stages—including Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B)—as well as 5 publicly traded space & aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to the next stage, implies a valuation of $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields a median of $1.1B, with a range of $274M to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable due to absence of revenue disclosure. The public space & aerospace sector trades at a median 59.8x price-to-sales, but without Aalyria's revenue on file, this method could not be deployed.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $914M to $2.8B, with a median of $1.8B. The current $1.3B valuation falls within this band, closer to the peer-median floor. Key uncertainties include competitive positioning within a crowded subsector, path to revenue, and the applicability of public-market multiples to pre-revenue or early-revenue private peers.
